libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use deterministic in-test property sources such as MapPropertySource, or built-in .properties/.props resources if testing file parsing. Avoid relying on ambient environment variables or JVM system properties unless the test sets and clears them explicitly.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Documentation examples commonly use YAML/JSON/TOML/HOCON files, but those formats require optional hoplite-* format modules; they are not required for meaningful hoplite-core coverage.
Hoplite 3 applies environment variables by default, so tests that use ConfigLoaderBuilder.default() with field names matching ambient variables may become nondeterministic.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: hoplite-core is an in-process Kotlin configuration library; the 3.0.0.RC1 artifact only brings Kotlin reflect/stdlib and coroutines dependencies, and meaningful core coverage is reachable with ConfigLoaderBuilder plus MapPropertySource or built-in .properties/.props parsing without external services, Docker, or required environment/system properties.
